question: If I provision a Cache-T1 instance for exactly 37 hours and transfer 135GB of data entirely within the same CloudScale region, what is my exact total cost?
expected_answer: Your total cost is $0.55. The Cache-T1 instance costs $0.015/hour (37 hours = $0.55), and data transfer within the same CloudScale region is completely free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA): $0.080/hour - Cache-Production (16GB RAM): $0.320/hour. Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: I am a 'Developer' in my organization. Can I invite a new contractor as a 'Viewer' to check logs, or do I need my 'Admin' to do it?
expected_answer: You need an Admin or Owner to do it. Developers cannot edit organization settings or member roles, nor can they invite new members.
reference_context: 5. Select a Role: - Owner: Full access, including billing and organization deletion. - Admin: Can manage all resources, settings, and invites, but cannot view billing or delete the organization. - Developer: Can manage applications, databases, and services, but cannot edit organization settings or member roles, nor invite members. - Viewer: Read-only access to all services and logs.

question: If I provision a Cache-T1 instance for exactly 13 hours and transfer 121GB of data entirely within the same CloudScale region, what is my exact total cost?
expected_answer: Your total cost is $0.20. The Cache-T1 instance costs $0.015/hour (13 hours = $0.20), and data transfer within the same CloudScale region is completely free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA): $0.080/hour - Cache-Production (16GB RAM): $0.320/hour. Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: Is public ingress (incoming traffic) billed at the standard $0.08 per GB rate like public egress?
expected_answer: No, network ingress (incoming data) is completely free of charge. Only public egress (outgoing data to the public internet) is billed at $0.08 per GB.
reference_context: Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: Is public ingress (incoming traffic) billed at the standard $0.08 per GB rate like public egress?

expected_answer: Yes, public egress (outgoing data to the public internet) is billed at $0.08 per GB, unlike network ingress which is free.
reference_context: Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: I am a 'Owner' in my organization. Can I invite a new backend engineer as a 'Developer' to check logs, or do I need my 'Admin' to do it?
expected_answer: You can do it. Owners have the permission to manage settings and invites.
reference_context: 5. Select a Role: - Owner: Full access, including billing and organization deletion. - Admin: Can manage all resources, settings, and invites, but cannot view billing or delete the organization. - Developer: Can manage applications, databases, and services, but cannot edit organization settings or member roles, nor invite members. - Viewer: Read-only access to all services and logs.

question: I am a 'Owner' in my organization. Can I invite a new frontend dev as a 'Developer' to check logs, or do I need my 'Admin' to do it?
expected_answer: You can do it. Owners have the permission to manage settings and invites.
reference_context: 5. question: If I provision a Cache-M1 instance for exactly 14 hours and transfer 193GB of data entirely within the local region, what is my exact total cost?
expected_answer: Your total cost is $1.12. The Cache-M1 instance costs $0.080/hour (14 hours = $1.12), and data transfer within the local region is completely free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA): $0.080/hour - Cache-Production (16GB RAM): $0.320/hour. Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

expected_answer: Yes, public egress (outgoing data to the public internet) is billed at $0.08 per GB, unlike network ingress which is free.
reference_context: Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: If I provision a Cache-Production instance for exactly 60 hours and transfer 284GB of data entirely within the same CloudScale region, what is my exact total cost?
expected_answer: Your total cost is $19.20. The Cache-Production instance costs $0.320/hour (60 hours = $19.20), and data transfer within the same CloudScale region is completely free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA): $0.080/hour - Cache-Production (16GB RAM): $0.320/hour. Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: My single-node PostgreSQL database failed. Will it automatically failover to a replica with zero downtime?
expected_answer: No. Single-node dedicated databases do not automatically failover because there is no replica. CloudScale will attempt crash recovery on a replacement node, which incurs downtime.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed PostgreSQL and MySQL take automated daily backups retained for 7 days. If a single-node database fails, CloudScale will automatically attempt crash recovery on a replacement node, incurring downtime. For zero-downtime failover, you must explicitly provision an HA (High Availability) replica. Failover to an HA replica is automatic and typically resolves in under 10 seconds.

question: How do I configure a custom domain name using the Enterprise Tier for my React frontend?
expected_answer: Since you are on the Enterprise Tier, you can configure a custom domain name for your React frontend by navigating to your app settings and adding the domain, as Enterprise supports custom domains and SSL.
reference_context: The Free Tier includes 1 app, shared compute, and a cloudscale.io subdomain. To use a custom domain name, SSL certificates, or Auto-Scaling, you must upgrade your organization to the Pro Tier or higher (Enterprise).

question: I am a 'Viewer' in my organization. Can I invite a new contractor as a 'Developer' to check logs, or do I need my 'Admin' to do it?
expected_answer: You need an Admin or Owner to do it. Viewers cannot edit organization settings or member roles, nor can they invite new members.
reference_context: 5. Select a Role: - Owner: Full access, including billing and organization deletion. - Admin: Can manage all resources, settings, and invites, but cannot view billing or delete the organization. - Developer: Can manage applications, databases, and services, but cannot edit organization settings or member roles, nor invite members. - Viewer: Read-only access to all services and logs.

question: If I provision a Cache-T1 instance for exactly 30 hours and transfer 42GB of data entirely within the local region, what is my exact total cost?
expected_answer: Your total cost is $0.45. The Cache-T1 instance costs $0.015/hour (30 hours = $0.45), and data transfer within the local region is completely free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA): $0.080/hour - Cache-Production (16GB RAM): $0.320/hour. Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA): $0.080/hour - Cache-Production (16GB RAM): $0.320/hour. question: My High Availability (HA) MySQL database failed. Will it automatically failover to a replica with zero downtime?
expected_answer: Yes. Because you have explicitly provisioned an HA (High Availability) replica, failover is automatic and typically resolves in under 10 seconds with near-zero downtime.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed PostgreSQL and MySQL take automated daily backups retained for 7 days. If a single-node database fails, CloudScale will automatically attempt crash recovery on a replacement node, incurring downtime. For zero-downtime failover, you must explicitly provision an HA (High Availability) replica. Failover to an HA replica is automatic and typically resolves in under 10 seconds.

reference_context: Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.
